articles.title: 👉 Use this link to GENERATE YOUR FIRST VIDEO FOR FREE 👈
OPENAI'S NEW SOCIAL APP IS FILLED WITH TERRIFYING SAM ALTMAN DEEPFAKES
articles.summary: Sora is already filled with deepfakes of Sam Altman in surreal scenarios, from serving Pikachu drinks to stealing Nvidia GPUs. The app's defining feature is a “cameo” system that lets users upload biometric data to generate realistic deepfakes of themselves and others. Despite parental controls and user permissions, Sora enables mass creation of highly convincing synthetic content.

articles.title: META TO USE AI CHATS TO PERSONALIZE CONTENT AND ADS FROM DECEMBER
articles.summary: Meta will begin using interactions with its AI tools to personalize content and ads on Facebook and Instagram starting December 16. The change applies only to those who use Meta AI, and users cannot opt out. Conversations through voice or text will be combined with existing data, such as likes and follows, to shape recommendations, though sensitive topics like religion, health, or political views will not be used for ads. Meta AI currently has 1B monthly active users. The rollout will exclude the UK, the European Union, and South Korea.

articles.title: EVALUATING THE IMPACT OF AI ON THE LABOR MARKET
articles.summary: This study found no evidence that AI has significantly disrupted the US labor market since ChatGPT's release in 2022. While the occupational mix has shifted slightly faster than in past periods, these changes largely predate gen AI and mirror historical patterns. Employment, unemployment, and industry-level shifts show no clear link to AI exposure, automation, or augmentation.

articles.title: KEEP VIDEO ADS UNDER 10 SECONDS
articles.summary: Video ads that are 10 seconds or shorter drive stronger results at a lower cost than longer ads. In a study of 59,988 TV ad airings and 25,000+ Facebook ad impressions, ≤10 second ads generated 3x more engagement on Facebook, up to 40% more website traffic on TV, and similar sales compared to longer ads. Shorter ads would also save $285,300 in airtime and generate 192,000 more site visits if aired 100 times on TV instead of 15 second ads. Effectiveness increases when brands are mentioned early, with traffic rising 34.1% compared to waiting until the end.

articles.title: HOW TO SUCCEED IN A ZERO CLICK WORLD
articles.summary: Many brands still optimize for SEO or REO while AI assistants give binary outcomes where a brand is either cited or invisible. For example, a press release can be ignored by ChatGPT if it is not structured correctly, even if it ranks well in Google. A better approach is to create schema-rich FAQs, semantic headlines, and backlinks to authority pages while earning mentions in media and forums. This ensures AI systems recognize the brand and include it in answers, not just rank it on a page.

articles.title: HOW I TURNED FORGOTTEN IDEAS INTO CONSISTENT POSTS
articles.summary: This guide outlines a 3-step content system. Step one is to post ideas you care about, not ones you feel forced to make. Step two is to build a habit of logging every idea immediately using tools like Notes. Step three is to keep it simple and repeatable with a workflow of create, edit, and post.

articles.title: REIMAGINING LINKEDIN GTM: AI WORKFLOWS, AGENTS & AUTOMATION (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: This webinar on October 8 will show how GTM teams can turn LinkedIn into a revenue channel using AI workflows and automations. It covers building an AI-assisted LinkedIn system, creating on-brand content, running a predictable lead-generation pipeline, establishing engagement routines, and automating funnel handoffs to convert reach into qualified leads.

articles.title: GMAIL'S LATEST UPDATES: WHAT'S CHANGING, WHY IT MATTERS, AND HOW TO ADAPT
articles.summary: Gmail is giving subscribers more control and surfacing emails based on engagement rather than send time. The new ‘Manage Subscriptions' view lists each sender with a snapshot of how often they send messages. The ‘Purchases' tab organizes receipts and delivery messages into a scannable format that rewards transactional clarity. In the ‘Promotions' tab, Gmail is using machine learning to push relevant offers to the top while generic campaigns drop out of view. These changes support a cleaner inbox and force email programs to compete on usefulness and timing.

articles.title: THE MECHANICS OF NEUROBRANDING: BRAND STRATEGY THAT STARTS IN THE FEELS
articles.summary: This article describes how to use neuroscience to build real brand preference. The first mechanic is immersion. Identify where users interact with the brand and make those moments emotionally rich. This could be a scented store, tactile packaging, or a sensory-rich waiting experience like Disney's Rise of the Resistance queue. The second mechanism is neural coupling. Brands that tell stories that reflect customer identity build loyalty without selling. The third mechanic is subconscious priming. Repetition of a distinct color, sound, or phrase wires emotional associations over time.

articles.title: A CRASH COURSE ON LAGGING AND LEADING METRICS
articles.summary: Lagging metrics measure outcomes but are slow to respond. Leading metrics give directional signals but need interpretation. Most teams over-index on lagging metrics like NPS or revenue per user because they feel more concrete and definitive. However, these often come too late to guide action. Marketers can build better dashboards by pairing each lagging metric with 2 to 3 leading ones. For example, tie conversion rate to DAUs or time to first value. This dual tracking helps steer strategy in real time and spot issues like churn or underused features quickly.

articles.title: SIGNAL MINING: HOW TO FIND “BITE YOUR HAND OFF” CUSTOMERS WITH AI
articles.summary: Use internal and public data sources to identify when your target audience has experienced multiple failed solutions, used emotional language, or tried broken workarounds.

articles.title: EMAIL FOOTER DESIGN: 9 EXAMPLES THAT ACTUALLY WORK
articles.summary: A well-designed footer includes navigation to key website sections, an unsubscribe link, social proof, and preference management.
